morta_nuts2
  • Mortality Table
  • View page source

Mortality Table

mortality_table

High-performance mortality data processing module.

This module provides an optimized pipeline to compute mortality metrics from raw demographic datasets (mortality rates, exposures, deaths).

Main features

  • Efficient vectorized computations using NumPy and pandas

  • Parallel processing for regional computations

  • Smart caching system to avoid recomputation

  • Automatic data validation with detailed reports

  • Flexible configuration via dataclass

  • Multi-format export (CSV, Parquet, Excel, Feather)

  • Compatibility with actuarial models (e.g., Lee-Carter, Lee-Li)

Typical workflow

  1. Load raw datasets (mxt, Lxt, Dxt)

  2. Initialize MortalityCalculator

  3. Run mortality computation

  4. Export or transform outputs for modeling

Example

>>> calc = MortalityCalculator()
>>> df = calc.calculate_mortality(mxt, Lxt, Dxt)
>>> calc.export_results(df, "output.csv")

Notes

This module is designed for actuarial and demographic applications, including mortality modeling and risk analysis.

class morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.MortalityConfig(age_max: int = 82, age_anomalies: ndarray = <factory>, fill_value: float = 1e-06, n_jobs: int = -1, enable_cache: bool = True, cache_dir: str | None = None, validate_data: bool = True, correct_anomalies: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration class for mortality calculations.

This class centralizes all configurable parameters used in the mortality computation pipeline.

Parameters:

  • age_max (int, default=82) – Maximum age considered in computations.

  • age_anomalies (np.ndarray) – Ages where mortality anomalies are corrected via interpolation.

  • fill_value (float, default=1e-6) – Value used to fill missing data in pivot tables.

  • n_jobs (int, default=-1) – Number of parallel jobs (-1 means automatic detection).

  • enable_cache (bool, default=True) – Whether to enable caching of intermediate results.

  • cache_dir (str, optional) – Directory where cache files are stored.

  • validate_data (bool, default=True) – Whether to validate input datasets before processing.

  • correct_anomalies (bool, default=True) – Whether to apply anomaly correction on mortality rates.

age_max: int = 82
age_anomalies: ndarray
fill_value: float = 1e-06
n_jobs: int = -1
enable_cache: bool = True
cache_dir: str | None = None
validate_data: bool = True
correct_anomalies: bool = True
class morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.DataValidator[source]

Bases: object

Utility class for validating input datasets.

Provides generic and domain-specific validation methods for mortality-related data.

static validate_dataframe(df: DataFrame, required_columns: List[str], name: str = 'DataFrame') → Dict[str, any][source]

Validates a DataFrame and returns a report.

static validate_mortality_data(mxt_raw: DataFrame, Lxt_raw: DataFrame, Dxt_raw: DataFrame) → Dict[str, any][source]

Specific validation for mortality data.

class morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.CacheManager(cache_dir: str, enabled: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Cache manager to optimize repeated calculations.

get(key: str) → any | None[source]

Retrieves a value from the cache.

set(key: str, value: any)[source]

Stores a value in the cache.

clear()[source]

Clears the cache.

morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.timing_decorator(func)[source]

Decorator to measure execution time.

class morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.MortalityCalculator(shapefile_path: str | None = None, config: MortalityConfig | None = None, auto_load_regions: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Optimized mortality calculator with advanced features.

Innovative features: - Smart cache to avoid recomputations - Automatic data validation - Multi-format export support - Configurable pipeline - Detailed logging - Robust error handling

DEFAULT_SHAPEFILE = WindowsPath('C:/Users/Idrissa Belem/Documents/GitHub/morta_nuts2/src/morta_nuts2/NUTS_files/NUTS_RG_01M_2024_3035.shp')
FRANCE_OUTREMER = ['FRY1', 'FRY2', 'FRY3', 'FRY4', 'FRY5']
__init__(shapefile_path: str | None = None, config: MortalityConfig | None = None, auto_load_regions: bool = True)[source]

Initializes the mortality calculator.

Parameters:

  • shapefile_path (str, optional) – Path to the NUTS shapefile. If None, uses the default path.

  • config (MortalityConfig, optional) – Custom configuration

  • auto_load_regions (bool, default=True) – Automatically loads regions from the shapefile

set_shapefile(shapefile_path: str)[source]

Changes the shapefile and reloads regions.

Parameters:

shapefile_path (str) – New path to the shapefile

get_regions(country: str = 'FR') → List[str][source]

Gets the list of regions filtered by country.

Parameters:

country (str, default="FR") – Country code (FR to filter overseas territories)

Returns:

List of region codes

Return type:

List[str]

validate_input_data(mxt_raw: DataFrame, Lxt_raw: DataFrame, Dxt_raw: DataFrame) → Dict[str, any][source]

Validates the input data.

Returns:

Validation report

Return type:

Dict

calculate_mortality(mxt_raw: DataFrame, Lxt_raw: DataFrame, Dxt_raw: DataFrame, regions: List[str] | None = None, gender: str = 'T', country: str = 'FR', aggregate_age: bool = False, use_cache: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]

Unified main method to calculate mortality.

Parameters:

  • mxt_raw (pd.DataFrame) – Mortality rate data

  • Lxt_raw (pd.DataFrame) – Exposure data

  • Dxt_raw (pd.DataFrame) – Deaths data

  • regions (List[str], optional) – List of regions to process. If None, uses self.regions

  • gender (str, default="T") – Gender (“T”, “M”, “F”)

  • country (str, default="FR") – Country code

  • aggregate_age (bool, default=True) – If True, aggregates results by age

  • use_cache (bool, default=True) – Uses cache if available

Returns:

Mortality calculation results

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

mortality_by_region(*args, **kwargs) → DataFrame[source]

Calculates mortality aggregated by region and year. Alias for calculate_mortality with aggregate_age=True.

mortality_by_region_by_age(*args, **kwargs) → DataFrame[source]

Calculates mortality by region, year and age. Alias for calculate_mortality with aggregate_age=False.

export_results(df: DataFrame, output_path: str, format: Literal['csv', 'parquet', 'excel', 'feather'] = 'csv', **kwargs)[source]

Exports results in different formats.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame to export

  • output_path (str) – Output path

  • format (str) – Export format (‘csv’, ‘parquet’, ‘excel’, ‘feather’)

  • **kwargs – Additional arguments for the export function

get_statistics() → Dict[str, any][source]

Returns execution statistics.

Returns:

Complete statistics

Return type:

Dict

clear_cache()[source]

Clears the cache.

summary()[source]

Displays a summary of the configuration and statistics.

morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.mortality_by_region(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Compatibility function - creates an instance and calculates.

morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.mortality_by_region_by_age(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Compatibility function - creates an instance and calculates.

morta_nuts2.data.mortality_table.build_input_from_dataframe(df)[source]

Transforms a long DataFrame into 3D matrices compatible with LCp_fit.

Parameters:

df (DataFrame with columns) – [‘region’, ‘year’, ‘age’, ‘deaths’, ‘exposure’,’mortality_rate’]

Returns:

  • Muxtg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • Dxtg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • Extg ((nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • xv (sorted age vector)

  • tv (sorted year vector)

  • regions (list of regions)
